sensoryProfile.sight: Pale walls, high towers, the palace dome taking the last of the sun, flower paste ground into cobbles, blue ribbon shreds in gutter water, and a vast banner reading WELCOME HOME, PRINCE ARTHAS above relaxed guards.
sensoryProfile.sound: Cheerful bells, wagon checks done in bored courtesy, and civic normalcy still running as if the kingdom had actually been saved.
sensoryProfile.smell: Autumn road dust, city stone, horse sweat, and the colder wrongness deepening near the palace.
sensoryProfile.touch: Soft Lordaeron damp outside the walls giving way to colder palace-adjacent stone as the group nears the inner court.
sensoryProfile.atmosphere: Capital City in chapter22 is triumph used as camouflage. The city’s public surface does more to hide Arthas than any ward or guard line.

establishedFacts.fact: Arthas has been in Capital City for four days by the time the protagonists arrive, and the public story still frames him as the prince who ended the threat.
Peacetime gate posture remains in effect even while Northrend guards settle into interior palace rotation.
By chapter24 the city surface has shifted from celebration into tense public aftermath, but the kingdom still tries to convert the throne-room catastrophe into a manageable legal event.

eventsHere.event: The formation rides through a celebrating city, meets Antonidas at the eastern service arch, and reaches the palace margins without raising public alarm.
Capital City absorbs the death of king and prince into tribunal process, prison security, and rumor, while outside reinforcements from Silvermoon begin arriving against that official story.
